_description: Pre-researched SEO topics for 404memoryfound.com. Each topic is a deep-dive narrative (3000-5000 words) written by one of two personas (Marcus or Dana). Generator picks 2 per run (1 Marcus + 1 Dana), top of each tier first. Cross off topics as published.
_strategy: Prioritized by search volume vs competition ratio. Tier 1 = high volume, very low long-form competition. Tier 2 = good volume, moderate competition but winnable with narrative depth. Tier 3 = niche authority builders with almost zero competition.

priority_1_high.title: What Happened to Neopets, the Virtual World That Raised a Generation
priority_1_high.angle: Viacom's $160M acquisition, Flash apocalypse, JumpStart mismanagement, 2023 management buyout. Rise-fall-resurrection business story. 200M+ accounts created.

priority_1_high.title: How Doom Changed Everything About PC Gaming and the Internet
priority_1_high.angle: Shareware model revolution ($100K/day from 1% conversion), id Software garage origins, Carmack's 3D engine, cultural panic over violence, LAN party catalyst.

priority_1_high.title: What Happened to Screensavers: Flying Toasters, 3D Pipes, and the Art of Doing Nothing
priority_1_high.angle: After Dark franchise ($50 for screensavers), Berkeley Systems, 3D Maze and Pipes in Windows, Johnny Castaway.

priority_1_high.title: What Happened to Club Penguin, Disney's Accidental MMO
priority_1_high.angle: 200M registered users, Disney's $350M acquisition (2007), the shutdown decision (2017), Club Penguin Island failure.
target_keywords: why did club penguin shut down

priority_1_high.title: What Happened to Clippy, the Paperclip Everyone Loved to Hate
priority_1_high.angle: Microsoft Bob predecessor, Office 97 launch, the user research they ignored, disabled by default in Office XP.

priority_1_high.title: The Original Sims: How a Virtual Dollhouse Became the Best-Selling PC Game Ever
priority_1_high.angle: Will Wright pitched it as architecture sim, EA almost killed it. Surprise demographic (majority female gamers in 2000). 200M+ franchise copies.

priority_1_high.title: What Happened to MiniDisc, Sony's Forgotten Music Format
priority_1_high.angle: Sony spent billions on development. Dominated Japan, flopped in US. DRM restrictions killed it before iPod did.

priority_1_high.title: What Happened to Polaroid, the Camera That Died and Came Back
priority_1_high.angle: Edwin Land's invention, $3B peak revenue, 2001 and 2008 bankruptcies, Impossible Project rescue mission buying the last factory.

priority_2_medium_high.title: What Happened to Kazaa and the P2P Wars After Napster
priority_2_medium_high.angle: Sharman Networks, FastTrack protocol, $100M RIAA settlement, Australian court case.

priority_2_medium_high.title: What Happened to Iomega Zip Drives and the Click of Death
priority_2_medium_high.angle: Stock went from $2 to $100 and back. The Click of Death defect and class-action lawsuit. 100MB felt infinite.

priority_2_medium_high.title: What Happened to SimCity and How EA Destroyed It
priority_2_medium_high.angle: Will Wright's original vision, SimCity 2000's perfection, SimCity 2013's always-online DRM disaster.

priority_2_medium_high.title: What Happened to RollerCoaster Tycoon, Built by One Man in Assembly
priority_2_medium_high.angle: Chris Sawyer single-handedly coded RCT in x86 assembly language. 4M+ copies of RCT1.

priority_3_niche_authority.title: What Happened to BonziBuddy, the Internet's Friendliest Spyware
priority_3_niche_authority.angle: The purple gorilla that harvested your data. FTC complaint, $75K fine.

priority_3_niche_authority.title: What Happened to Oregon Trail, the Game That Taught America to Die of Dysentery
priority_3_niche_authority.angle: Created in 1971 by three student teachers on a teletype. MECC distributed it to every school. 65M+ copies.

priority_3_niche_authority.title: What Happened to Newgrounds, the Internet Before YouTube
priority_3_niche_authority.angle: Tom Fulp's creation, Flash animation golden age, Salad Fingers, Alien Hominid going retail.

priority_3_niche_authority.title: What Happened to IRC, the Chat Protocol That Built the Internet
priority_3_niche_authority.angle: Created by Jarkko Oikarinen in 1988. Gulf War was first event covered on IRC.

priority_3_niche_authority.title: What Happened to the Nokia 3310, the Phone That Refused to Die
priority_3_niche_authority.angle: Nokia 3310 launched September 2000, sold 126M units, became the indestructible-phone meme. HMD Global revival at MWC 2017. Snake II, Xpress-on covers, week-long battery life as the unintended cultural symbol.

priority_3_niche_authority.title: What Happened to NCSA Mosaic, the Browser That Built the World Wide Web
priority_3_niche_authority.angle: NCSA Mosaic released Jan 1993 by Andreessen and Bina at U Illinois. IMG tag controversy with Berners-Lee. Spyglass licensing chain to Microsoft IE for $2M+$8M settlement. Distribution-over-invention lesson.
